assertions.title: Users can traverse directly between nodes while retaining visible graph context

steps.action: Open /#graph-roadmap with the Roadmap atlas focused
steps.expected: The roadmap shell shows nearby graph controls that allow node-to-node traversal without leaving the graph-first surface.
steps.observed: The Roadmap state loaded with both the Related control row and the ATLAS minimap row visible above the focused roadmap content.

steps.action: Activate the visible Tokenomics minimap node from the graph-context minimap
steps.expected: The experience should move directly to the Tokenomics node while keeping surrounding graph context visible on screen.
steps.observed: The URL updated to #graph-tokenomics and the Tokenomics node became the active atlas state while the Related row and minimap remained pinned in view above the destination content.

steps.action: Inspect the post-transition viewport for retained graph context
steps.expected: Users should still see graph context/minimap/overview rather than collapsing into a document-only panel.
steps.observed: The post-transition viewport still showed the Related pills and the ATLAS minimap row, with measured bounds remaining on screen (related controls top 72.75-98.75px, minimap top 123.75-144.25px) while Tokenomics content rendered below them.

evidence.network: No failing requests observed; this hash-only node transition did not produce additional network traffic in agent-browser.

frictions.description: A generic role-based click on the visible 'Tokenomics' control matched multiple buttons because the page exposes the same destination in overview cards, related pills, and the minimap.
frictions.resolution: Retested with an explicit selector for the visible minimap node inside data-testid='graph-context-minimap' so the captured traversal matches the retained-context control under test.

summary: Retested 1 assertion: VAL-ROADMAP-012 passed. Traversing from Roadmap to Tokenomics via the visible minimap kept the surrounding graph context on screen instead of collapsing into a document-only view.
